question_text: One wine category introduced DOCG planting rules requiring newly established vineyards to use espalier training and a minimum density of 4,000 plants per hectare. The other had a town formally admitted in 2001 as a sub-area within a larger DOC. Which pairing matches those two classification details?

source_facts:

---
[2] In 2001, the town of Nizza was officially recognized as a sub-region within the greater Barbera d'Asti DOC.

question_text: A vineyard manager wants to reduce the number of weed seeds in the soil of their organic vineyard. In what order should they apply the following treatments to most effectively achieve this goal?
options: [{'id': 'A', 'text': 'Apply mulch followed by tillage'}, {'id': 'B', 'text': 'Tillage followed by irrigation'}, {'id': 'C', 'text': 'Irrigation followed by tillage'}, {'id': 'D', 'text': 'Apply mulch followed by irrigation'}]
source_facts:
[1] Apply products as directed sprays to the base of woody stems and trunks.
---
[2] A summer fallow treatment of irrigation (to encourage weed seed germination) followed by tillage (to uproot newly emerged weed seedlings) will desiccate weeds and reduce the number of weed seeds in the soil.
---
[3] Organic mulches breakdown with time and the original thickness is typically reduced by 60% after one year.

question_text: A winemaker in a German wine village is preparing labels for a new Riesling release harvested from a specific vineyard site. question_text: A merchant house specializing in blending wine and sherry was established in 1796 in Bristol, England by a founder named William Perry, and has since operated under a different trading name. Which business is being described?
options: [{'id': 'A', 'text': 'Calleón de los Bolos'}, {'id': 'B', 'text': 'Can Bonastre'}, {'id': 'C', 'text': 'Bodegas Toro Albalá'}, {'id': 'D', 'text': 'John Harvey & Sons'}]

---

---
[3] John Harvey & Sons is a brand (trading name) of a wine and sherry blending and merchant business founded by William Perry in Bristol, England in 1796.
---
[4] La Cańa is a wine producer in the Galicia region of Spain.
---
[5] Pago de Otazu is a branch of Bodega Otazu, a Spanish winery in Navarre, Spain.
---
[6] Calleón de los Bolos winery is located in Jerez de la Frontera, Spain.

question_text: A viticulturist notes that one grape variety has notably thick skin, giving it meaningful protection against downy mildew pressure that can strike late in the growing season. Which grape does this description fit, when contrasted with a late-ripening variety typically picked in early to mid-October whose growers are now trialing techniques to pull the harvest earlier?
options: [{'id': 'A', 'text': 'Both varieties share thick skins and late-October ripening'}, {'id': 'B', 'text': 'Grechetto, with Nebbiolo as the late-ripening counterpart'}, {'id': 'C', 'text': 'Nebbiolo, with Grechetto as the late-ripening counterpart'}, {'id': 'D', 'text': 'Neither variety is known for mildew resistance or late ripening'}]
source_facts:
[1] Grechetto 's thick skin provides good resistance to downy mildew which  can attack the grape late in the harvest season.
---
[2] The harvest of the late-ripening nebbiolo grape usually takes place in early to mid-October though some producers are experimenting with viticultural techniques that allow for an earlier harvest in late.
